name: Far Cry® 5
description: Discover the open world of Hope County, Montana, besieged by a fanatical doomsday cult. Dive into the action solo or two-player co-op in the story campaign, use a vast arsenal of weapons and allies, and free Hope County from Joseph Seed and his cult.
requirements:
Minimum:
  • Requires a 64-bit processor and operating system
  • OS: Windows 10 (64-bit versions only)
  • Processor: Intel Core i5-2400 @ 3.1 GHz or AMD FX-6300 @ 3.5 GHz or equivalent
  • Memory: 8 GB RAM
  • Graphics: NVIDIA GeForce GTX 670 or AMD R9 270 (2GB VRAM with Shader Model 5.0 or better)
  • DirectX: Version 9.0c
  • Network: Broadband Internet connection
  • Storage: 40 GB available space
  • Additional Notes: Game contains EasyAntiCheat anti-cheat technology, and Denuvo and VMProtect anti-tamper technology.
comment1:
The reason is simple: the game stopped working correctly.

The problem: It takes forever to load a game, something that didn't happen with my current PC.

And Ubisoft is once again releasing poorly made games that stop working over time. It happened to me with Assassin's Creed Unity, then with Ghost Recon Wildlands. The former literally crashed when I walked around the environment, while the latter had the same problem as Far Cry 5.
Solution: There isn't one. In fact, you'd have to mess with your motherboard's BIOS to disable multithreading to make it work, and even then, not perfectly.

If you're going to buy this game—because let's be honest, it's good—you're playing Russian roulette. It might not work for you. Fortunately, Steam allows refunds, so I recommend you try it. If it loads quickly, great, you don't have that problem. But if it takes more than 4 to 5 minutes with an SSD, you should request a refund immediately.

It's a real shame that such a big developer abandons its games without support, leaving us, the users, to fix things they should have done themselves. I guess we're to blame for patching them, when the best thing to do is just stop buying from them. Even if you're thinking of buying a good game, if it says "(Created by Ubisoft)," just ignore it. You'll save yourself a lot of headaches and money.
comment2: Far Cry 5 is easily my favorite Far Cry game ever made. The story was decent and felt believable enough to stay engaging, but the real star of the game for me was the setting. I’ve never personally been to Montana, but this game makes it feel exactly how I’d imagine it to be. The nature, the towns, the people, the atmosphere... everything about it feels beautiful and immersive. Even after fully completing it, it’s still a game I could easily come back to just because of how much I love the world. Easy 10/10 for me.
comment3: Unable to play the game because,Ubisoft connect...
